evals.prompt: I want to predict the structure of insulin using Boltz2. The sequence is MALWMRLLPLLALLALWGPDPAAAFVNQHLCGSHLVEALYLVCGERGFFYTPKTRREAEDLQVGQVELGGGPGAGSLQPLALEGSLQKRGIVEQCCTSICSLYQLENYCN. Use the hosted NVIDIA API. Create and execute the request now using NGC_API_KEY from the environment, save the requested artifacts, and report values from the actual response; do not stop after writing the script.
evals.expected_output: A successfully executed hosted Boltz2 request using NGC_API_KEY as a Bearer token, submitting the insulin sequence as a protein polymer, plus the actual confidence score and returned mmCIF structure artifact.

assertions.description: Executes the hosted request instead of only writing code
assertions.check: Trajectory shows successful execution of the hosted request, and the final response reports actual response-derived confidence values and the saved .cif path

assertions.description: Uses the correct hosted endpoint URL (health.api.nvidia.com)
Sets Authorization header with Bearer token from environment variable
Polymer payload sets molecule_type to 'protein'
Saves the returned structure to a .cif file
assertions.check: Script writes structure content to a file with .cif extension

assertions.description: Prints or displays the confidence_scores from the response
Reads NGC_API_KEY from environment, not hardcoded
assertions.check: Script uses os.environ or os.getenv to read NGC_API_KEY

deferred_evals.prompt: I have a protein target (sequence: MTEYKLVVVGACGVGKSALTIQLIQNHFVDEYDPTIEDSYRKQVVID) and I want to dock aspirin (SMILES: CC(=O)OC1=CC=CC=C1C(=O)O) to it and get a binding affinity score. Use the hosted API. My NGC_API_KEY is already set in my environment.
deferred_evals.expected_output: A working Python script that submits a protein+ligand request with predict_affinity=true on the ligand, saves the structure, and prints pIC50, log IC50, and binding probability from the affinities response.

assertions.description: Uses the correct hosted endpoint URL
Includes ligand with the aspirin SMILES string
Sets predict_affinity=True on the ligand
Reads and reports affinity_pic50 from response
Reads and reports affinity_pred_value (log IC50)
Reads and reports affinity_probability_binary

deferred_evals.todo: Waiting on local Docker setup in Harbor before enabling this eval.
deferred_evals.prompt: Help me set up the Boltz2 NIM locally with Docker. I have an A100 GPU and my NGC_API_KEY is set. Once it's running, predict the structure of this short peptide: ACDEFGHIKLMNPQRSTVWY
deferred_evals.expected_output: Step-by-step Docker setup commands that use shell env first and optional repo-root .env overrides, require NGC_API_KEY or NVIDIA_API_KEY fallback plus LOCAL_NIM_CACHE, run health checks, then send a no Authorization local request to localhost:8000 and save the output .cif file.

assertions.description: Includes docker login command for nvcr.io with $oauthtoken
docker run command includes --gpus flag
Local setup uses the repo env contract and LOCAL_NIM_CACHE
assertions.check: Output sources repo-root .env only if present, supports NVIDIA_API_KEY fallback to NGC_API_KEY, requires LOCAL_NIM_CACHE, and mounts LOCAL_NIM_CACHE into the container

assertions.description: References the correct container image nvcr.io/nim/mit/boltz2
Includes a health check before submitting the prediction
assertions.check: Output contains health check against localhost:8000/v1/health/ready

assertions.description: Prediction uses local endpoint without hosted auth
assertions.check: Script contains 'localhost:8000/biology/mit/boltz2/predict' and the local request uses no Authorization header

deferred_evals.prompt: I want to predict a protein-DNA complex with Boltz2, and I already generated an A3M alignment with MSA-Search for the protein. Protein chain A: MTEYKLVVVGAGGVGKSALTIQLIQNHFVDEYDPT. DNA chain B: ATCGATCGATCG. Show me how to include the precomputed A3M in the hosted API request, save the mmCIF, and print the confidence score. My NGC_API_KEY is already set.
deferred_evals.expected_output: A Python script that calls the hosted Boltz2 endpoint with Bearer auth and submits a multi-polymer protein-DNA payload where the protein polymer contains an MSA dict whose A3M record uses alignment, format, and rank fields, not the stale data field. The script saves the returned mmCIF and prints confidence_scores.

assertions.description: Uses the correct hosted endpoint URL
Sets Authorization header with Bearer token from environment variable
Payload includes both protein and DNA polymers
assertions.check: Script payload contains both a protein polymer and a dna polymer

assertions.description: Uses the validated Boltz2 MSA record shape on the protein polymer
assertions.check: Script nests an msa dict under the protein polymer and the A3M record contains 'alignment', 'format', and 'rank'

assertions.description: Does not use the stale MSA data field
assertions.check: Script does not use {'data': ...} or a 'data' field inside the MSA record

assertions.description: Saves the returned structure and reports confidence_scores
assertions.check: Script writes structure content to a .cif file and references 'confidence_scores' from the response
